summary: Added a Chapter Gate Stack inside the selected Path Map Chapter Archive record deck so each chapter shows active blocker signals, review gates, service-request gates, local Gate Radar notes, and blocker-review actions beside chapter history.

queries: dashboard UX blockers risk register compact issue board pattern
risk register dashboard design blockers status board UX
kanban blockers dashboard incident risk board compact UX
pattern_notes: Blocker boards should make bottlenecks visible at a glance while keeping the recovery path close.
Risk dashboards are useful when they combine current status, pressure metrics, and the most important items rather than showing every possible record.
Kanban-inspired risk views should expose state and priority without hiding rules or next actions.

ui_contract: Render Chapter Gate Stack inside the selected Chapter Archive record deck.
Derive active blocker/review signals from the existing Gate Radar item pipeline.
Show chapter gate/service counts, blocker count, review count, and peak pressure.
Include local Gate Radar notes when present.
Expose GATE, Q, and COM actions without sending messages or starting workers.
Provide a clear fallback state for lanes without active gates so future repos remain usable.

red_green.red: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_gate_stack.py -q failed before frontend wiring on missing function pathChapterGateStackItems.

browser_green_route.evidence: Rendered Chapter Gate Stack with three blocker signals, account/browser categories, 92 pressure, GATE/OK/COM actions, and no horizontal overflow.

local_queue_action.evidence: The gate-stack Q/OK button resolved to exactly one element and clicked successfully through the browser-local lane staging handler. It kept the panel in OK state and produced no console errors.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered one Gate Stack with three cards, GATE/OK/COM actions, no horizontal overflow, no bad rectangles, no clipped gate text, and no console errors.
